rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, constants, and assignments
Write output range guarantees using visible feature names and case-provided bounds
Relate outputs to inputs in component type annexes without using implementation-only subcomponent paths
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations
generic_rules: Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
Align helper declaration types with the expression types
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree blocks
Use eq for equations, const for constants, and assign for assignments
Use bool for comparison expressions, int for integer arithmetic, and real for real arithmetic
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
